perspectives.title: AI-native transformation is a workflow problem before it is a model problem
perspectives.summary: Why the first bottleneck is usually ownership, escalation paths, and decision rights.

perspectives.title: The enterprise agent stack needs governance by design
perspectives.summary: Trust, auditability, permissions, and rollback should be product surfaces, not compliance afterthoughts.

perspectives.title: What investors should watch in enterprise AI adoption
perspectives.summary: Signals that separate novelty usage from durable organizational capability.

cases.label: Case 01
cases.title: AI-native knowledge operations for a complex service organization
cases.summary: Mapped fragmented knowledge workflows, designed retrieval and review loops, and defined a path from expert-dependent operations to reusable institutional memory.
points: Problem: tacit knowledge trapped across teams and tools
Approach: RAG workflow, source governance, human review checkpoints
Outcome: faster research cycles and clearer accountability for generated answers

cases.label: Case 02
cases.title: Agent-assisted operating model for sales and customer success
cases.summary: Translated scattered customer signals into a structured AI workflow for account preparation, follow-up generation, and escalation detection.
points: Problem: high-value context lost between meetings, CRM notes, and support logs
Approach: workflow agent, confidence thresholds, role-based handoff design
Outcome: better preparation quality and more consistent customer touchpoints

cases.label: Case 03
cases.title: Executive AI transformation roadmap and pilot portfolio
cases.summary: Helped prioritize where AI should change operating leverage first, separating attractive demos from initiatives with clear adoption and measurement paths.
points: Problem: too many AI ideas, too little transformation discipline
Approach: opportunity scoring, risk map, pilot portfolio design
Outcome: clearer sequencing for leadership, product, and implementation teams
